title: RFC: Auto-Detect Prefetch Depth by Mount Type
bodyMarkdown:
Follow-up from HEL-482, the incident where a 128-shard restore stalled after manifest load because the loader's default prefetch depth of 16 saturated an NFS mount.

## Background
src/checkpoint/loader.ts warms the shard cache by reading HELIOS_PREFETCH_DEPTH from config, defaulting to 16. That default is correct for local SSD and wrong for network storage, and nothing today tells an operator which regime they are in until a restore stalls.

## Proposal
Detect the mount type of the checkpoint directory at startup, by checking the filesystem type reported by the OS, and pick a sane default automatically: 16 for local disk, 4 for anything that looks like NFS or another network filesystem. HELIOS_PREFETCH_DEPTH would still override the detected value for operators who know better.

## Open questions
Container environments sometimes report the host filesystem type incorrectly, we need a fallback that errs conservative rather than defaulting back to 16 on ambiguous input. Also unresolved: whether this belongs in src/config/env.ts or as a new module in src/fs.

## Status
Not yet implemented. Filed as a follow-up from the incident, no committed timeline. Priya Natarajan is driving.
